BRA|NOR.text: Brazil face Norway in the World Cup round of 16, with the Norwegians appearing at the tournament for the first time in 28 years and seeking a maiden quarter-final berth. The tie features a subplot from the Premier League, as Manchester City's Erling Haaland and Arsenal defender Gabriel extend their club rivalry to the international stage.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
Brazil vs. Norway: Gabriel and Haaland take rivalry to international stage
Stay humble - the Premier League feud about to hit world stage

ENG|MEX.text: Jarell Quansah is set to start for England, with Thomas Tuchel and Jordan Henderson having dismissed Mexican media claims about players using Viagra to combat the altitude. Mexican fans gathered outside England's hotel through the early hours, blasting horns and setting off fireworks in an attempt to disrupt the squad's sleep. A shelter-in-place order was issued at the Azteca before kickoff due to severe weather.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night; Brazil face Norway
Jarell Quansah to start for England vs. Mexico at World Cup - sources
Mexico fans' serenata outside England hotel woke me up, and probably Three Lions too

BEL|USA.text: Folarin Balogun is available for the United States after his one-game ban was suspended by FIFA. Belgium reach this last-16 tie having eliminated Senegal in the previous round. Projection models give the USA a narrow edge to advance at 54%-46%.

sources.headline: USMNT World Cup bracket scenarios, odds to advance, predicted path to knockouts
Senegal delegation members still awaiting flight home five days after World Cup elimination - sources
Trump thanks Fifa after USA striker Balogun's ban suspended
